title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body: Updated the export functionality to support AI-generated masks. The function now handles mask data from JSON files, including AI-generated mask formats (list/array and base64 encoded images).

step_plan.summary: Normalize AI-generated masks (base64 images and JSON arrays) during JSON load into 2D boolean numpy arrays and update shapes_to_label to use provided masks directly instead of rasterizing polygon points, preserving AI-generated segmentation masks during export.

steps.operation: Normalize and robustly parse shape['mask'] when loading JSON
steps.reason: LabelFile.load must accept AI-generated masks stored in JSON in multiple formats (base64-encoded images as strings, and nested lists/arrays) and normalize them into a 2D boolean numpy array so downstream code (shapes_to_label) can use masks directly instead of rasterizing polygons.
steps.side_effects: Changes how JSON shape masks are interpreted: existing workflows that relied on previous decoding behavior may see different boolean masks if inputs were non-standard; unit tests for LabelFile.load and JSON import/export must be updated. If decoded/provided masks have dimensions that don't match the expected image size, downstream consumers (export_json, shapes_to_label) may raise or produce incorrect outputs; include clear logging/warnings on mismatch. Importing numpy (np) in labelme/label_file.py may be necessary; no public API signature changes but serialized JSON semantics change.

steps.operation: Prefer supplied boolean masks in shapes_to_label instead of always rasterizing points
steps.reason: shapes_to_label currently always rasterizes shapes via shape_to_mask. For AI-generated masks that are provided in the shape dict (normalized to a 2D boolean numpy array by LabelFile.load), shapes_to_label must use that mask directly so exports preserve AI-generated segmentations rather than re-rasterizing possibly-inaccurate polygons.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral change in label image generation and export: exported label and instance images will reflect provided masks. Tests covering shapes_to_label and export_json should be updated/extended to include mask-containing shapes. If supplied masks have mismatched dtype or dimensions, shapes_to_label must coerce via np.asarray(...).astype(bool) and should log/warn on shape mismatch; mismatched shapes can cause indexing errors or incorrect label maps. No external API signature changes, but downstream consumers that expect masks to be generated from points may observe different outputs.
